AMBIGUOUS_SKILLS_TO_REVIEW.label: cycle de développement logiciel
AMBIGUOUS_SKILLS_TO_REVIEW.rationale: Statistically spread (df=31.8%, 6 clusters, ratio=1.10) but label is intrinsically domain (DATA_IT). Apparent genericness likely comes from alias over-injection upstream, not true transversality.
AMBIGUOUS_SKILLS_TO_REVIEW.risk_if_misclassified: Tagging as generic would drop a legitimate DATA_IT / MKT / FIN signal on genuinely-domain candidates. Product review required.

AMBIGUOUS_SKILLS_TO_REVIEW.label: programmation informatique
AMBIGUOUS_SKILLS_TO_REVIEW.rationale: df=30.4%, but 86% concentrated in DATA_IT (ratio=1.89) → behaves as domain within its cluster; not generic. Listed as ambiguous only because high df makes naive filters flag it.
AMBIGUOUS_SKILLS_TO_REVIEW.risk_if_misclassified: Removing it would destroy the scoring signal within its native cluster (e.g. programmation informatique for DATA_IT candidates).
